title: Retail Customer Support Context Strategy
revision_notes: v0.2.0: Exam-guide / Playbook / cookbook alignment pass. Token-counting mechanics softened (Exam Guide out-of-scopes tokenization specifics) — AC-02.4 now grades consistency not algorithm; AC-08.1 replaced AST `len // 4` ban with consistency check. AC-02 gains AC-02.5 documenting chosen methodology in README. AC-08.6 'Why this layout?' must address the pass-complete-history tension and use the word 'scratchpad' for the Playbook synonym. AC-08.8 'What I'd do next' must name server-side context-editing betas (`clear_tool_uses_20250919`, `compact_20260112`). NG-10 (Claude Code /compact, Module 8) and NG-11 (server-side context-editing betas — application-side is the LO contrast) added. OQ-1 resolved as informational; OQ-7 added on app-side vs server-side; OQ-8 added on Haiku vs Sonnet default.

exam_guide_alignment.domain_weight_supported: Domain 5 (15% of scored content) — cross-cutting foundation

learning_objective_coverage: LO 2 — Explain harness assembly / maintenance / compression
LO 4 — Apply 'lost in the middle' to placement
LO 5 — Extract case facts into persistent block
LO 7 — Position key findings at boundaries with section headers
definition_of_done: pytest green for the story's test files
mypy or pyright green for retail_context/
changes committed with a message referencing the story ID

stories.title: Project scaffold runs in Docker
stories.benefit: I can start building without fighting environment setup

acceptance_criteria.text: docker compose up (or docker build && docker run) starts the container without error
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is read from env; container fails fast with a clear message if missing
python -m retail_context --version prints a semver string and exits 0
pyproject.toml pins anthropic to a specific version
pytest reports at least one passing smoke test (test_hello_claude)
mypy or pyright passes on retail_context/

stories.title: Transcript loader and baseline token accounting
stories.want: to load the 48-turn transcript and measure its baseline token cost
stories.benefit: every later compression step has a concrete number to beat

acceptance_criteria.text: retail_context.transcript.load(path) returns a typed Transcript with turns, segments, and token_count
fixture data/transcript_48turns.json has exactly 48 turns partitioned into refund (1–14), subscription (15–28), payment_update (29–48)
baseline token_count for the full transcript is between 42,000 and 52,000

token counting uses a single canonical retail_context.tokens.count function invoked consistently for every measurement (baseline, per-section, post-compression); algorithm choice is the learner's (Anthropic count_tokens, tiktoken, or documented deterministic estimator) — Exam Guide out-of-scopes tokenization specifics, so we grade methodology not arithmetic
chosen token-counting methodology is documented in README in one sentence so the reviewer can interpret the reported numbers
test_transcript_loader asserts turn count, partition boundaries, and token_count range

stories.title: Case-facts extraction into a persistent block
stories.want: structured transactional facts pulled into a single block at the top of context
stories.benefit: the model never scans 47k tokens to recover an order ID, refund amount, or payment status
exam_alignment: Task 5.1 Skills: 'case facts' extraction into persistent block

acceptance_criteria.text: retail_context.case_facts.extract(transcript) returns a CaseFacts model with the 12 required fields
extraction is either deterministic or a logged Claude call (input+output written to runs/<run_id>/case_facts_call.json) using claude-haiku-4-5 by default
serialized case-facts block is <= 600 tokens
block is rendered as human-readable Markdown with explicit section headers and a fixed key order
test_case_facts_extraction asserts every required field is populated and non-null
missing-field behavior: raises CaseFactExtractionError listing the missing fields — does not silently fill with null or 'unknown'

stories.title: Tool-output pruning for verbose lookup_order responses
stories.want: lookup_order's 40+ field response trimmed to the 5 fields relevant for return-eligibility reasoning
stories.benefit: verbose tool outputs do not balloon context as the conversation accumulates
exam_alignment: Task 5.1 Skills: trim verbose tool outputs to relevant fields

acceptance_criteria.text: retail_context.pruner.prune_lookup_order(raw) returns a dict with exactly the 5 required keys, in order, and no others
data/lookup_order_response.json contains >= 40 distinct top-level fields
pruner is deterministic field selection, not an LLM call (no anthropic import in pruner.py)
pruned output is <= 200 tokens
test_prune_lookup_order asserts kept set is exactly {order_id, order_date, order_total_usd, fulfillment_status, return_eligible_until} and that missing-field input raises PrunerMissingFieldError
pruner module docstring explains why each of the 5 kept fields matters for return/refund reasoning

stories.title: Tiered compression: resolved issues summarized, active issue verbatim
stories.want: resolved issues compressed into short factual summaries while the active issue is preserved verbatim
stories.benefit: the model has high-fidelity context where decisions are still being made and low-cost context where they are not
exam_alignment: Task 5.1 Skills: compression strategy — summarize vs preserve verbatim

acceptance_criteria.text: retail_context.compressor.summarize_segment(segment) produces a <= 500-token factual summary for refund and subscription segments
each summary contains an opening one-sentence outcome, 3–6 bulleted decision-relevant facts, and a closing one-sentence resolution
compression uses claude-haiku-4-5 by default, model configurable; prompt template lives at retail_context/prompts/compression_prompt.md for audit
active payment_update segment is never summarized — test_active_segment_preserved_verbatim asserts byte-exact preservation
combined token cost of both resolved summaries is <= 1,000 tokens (>= 95% reduction on the resolved portion)
total API spend for one full compression pass is <= $0.10 estimated from token counts and Haiku pricing

stories.title: Position-aware context assembly
stories.want: case facts at the top, resolved summaries in the middle, active issue immediately above the new user turn
stories.benefit: the 'lost in the middle' attention effect works in our favor
exam_alignment: Task 5.1 Skills: position-aware structuring — key findings at boundaries

acceptance_criteria.text: retail_context.assemble.build(transcript) produces context.markdown with sections in exact order: # Case Facts → # Resolved: Refund inquiry → # Resolved: Subscription cancellation → # Active issue: Payment-method update
total assembled token count is <= 24,000 tokens (>= 50% reduction from baseline)
active-issue section body is byte-exact equal to raw turns 29–48 concatenated with original turn headers
runs/<run_id>/context.md is written to disk as the canonical reviewer artifact
assembler does not interleave resolved summaries with active text — section boundaries are exclusive
runs/<run_id>/budget.json records baseline_tokens, assembled_tokens, reduction_pct (computed, not hardcoded), and per-section token counts

stories.title: Evaluation: compressed context preserves answerability
stories.want: a fixed set of evaluation questions answered against the compressed context
stories.benefit: I can grade whether the compression strategy preserved the information the agent will need

acceptance_criteria.text: python -m retail_context.run --eval loads the assembled context and issues one Claude call per question with the assembled context as system prompt
per-question results written to runs/<run_id>/eval.jsonl with question_id, question, expected_fragment, model_answer, passed, input_tokens, output_tokens
a question passes when expected_fragment.lower() is a substring of model_answer.lower()
pass threshold: >= 5/6 questions pass

control variant (case-facts block removed) makes at least one of Q1/Q6 fail. Q6 is the strict test — its expected fragment 'in_progress' appears only in the case-facts block; Q1's behavior depends on whether the resolved-refund summary preserved the numeric value verbatim. Q6 must fail; Q1 failing is bonus evidence.
total API spend for one --eval pass (6 questions + 2 control) is <= $0.25

eval_questions.question: What was the actual refund amount processed for order ORD-77310?
Why did the customer cancel their subscription?
What is the failure code on the current payment-method update?
What is the last-4 of the new card the customer is trying to add?
Did the customer receive their subscription proration refund?
What is the structured status of the payment-method update issue (exact status token)?

stories.title: Anti-pattern audit + README writeup
stories.want: automated checks plus a README writeup confirming Module 2 anti-patterns are avoided and the rationale is articulated

acceptance_criteria.text: pytest tests/test_antipatterns.py passes — static check that every token count in runs/<run_id>/budget.json is produced by the single canonical retail_context.tokens.count function (consistency, not algorithm, is enforced)
AST check that pruner.py contains no anthropic client import (deterministic, not LLM-driven)
static check that assemble.py builds sections in order Case Facts → Resolved → Active (regex on assembled output)
static check that verbatim active segment in context.md matches raw turns 29–48 byte-for-byte
README contains a context-window anatomy diagram showing system prompt → CLAUDE.md → memory → conversation history → current turn

README 'Why this layout?' section (4–6 sentences) explains position choices via 'lost in the middle' (LO 4), resolved-vs-active fidelity tradeoff (LO 2), AND explicitly addresses the pass-complete-history tension (Exam Task 5.1 Knowledge bullet); one sentence must use the word 'scratchpad' to recognize the Playbook synonym for the case-facts block
README before/after token table pulled from runs/<run_id>/budget.json; token-counting methodology named in one sentence

README 'What I'd do next' section (4–6 bullets) names at least: (a) session resumption (Module 9 preview), and (b) server-side context-editing betas as the modern API-level alternative — clear_tool_uses_20250919 (beta context-management-2025-06-27) and compact_20260112 (beta compact-2026-01-12) — contrasting when application-side pruning is preferable vs when to let the API manage it
README links eval results (eval.jsonl) and shows the per-question pass/fail table for one graded run

pruned_lookup_order_token_cost.note: verified by test_pruner; not measured in pipeline

control_eval_q6_fails_without_case_facts.actual: Q6 fails (Q1 passes — refund summary preserved $22.14 verbatim)

api_spend_per_full_pipeline_run_usd.note: ran via Claude Code CLI subscription; SDK billing path not exercised

stories_passing.actual: 7/8 (US-01 Docker scaffold deferred per build directive)

open_questions.text: Token counter algorithm — any consistent methodology works; Exam Guide out-of-scopes tokenization specifics
open_questions.resolution: informational — learner documents choice in README; no scaffolding decision required

open_questions.text: Case-facts extraction: Claude call vs deterministic parse of annotated fixture
open_questions.lean: Claude extraction — extraction is the LO; cost bounded by US-03 ≤ $0.10

open_questions.text: Ship a 'lost in the middle' negative variant (case facts placed mid-history) to quantify the position effect?
Active-issue boundary fixed at turn 29 vs learner-detected — detection is richer but drifts into Module 9
open_questions.lean: fixed via fixture annotation for Module 2

open_questions.text: Compression prompt template — provide starter or require learners to write their own?
open_questions.lean: provide starter; strategy is the LO, not prompt-craft

open_questions.text: Run Q3/Q4 against a variant where active is also summarized, to show fidelity loss?

Application-side pruning (this project) vs server-side context editing (clear_tool_uses_20250919, compact_20260112) — the Module 2 cookbooks teach server-side; this project teaches the application-side counterpart Exam Task 5.1 emphasizes ('before they accumulate in context'). Ship optional appendix lab comparing the two?
open_questions.scope: stand-out suggestion — would strengthen LO 2 + LO 6 contrast

open_questions.text: Default model: Haiku 4.5 (keeps project < $0.50/run) vs Sonnet 4.6 (matches Module 2 cookbook labs, higher-fidelity summaries)
open_questions.lean: Haiku default; learners may override via --model
